penalty_breakdown.title: Permission policy is not enforced on all dispatch paths
penalty_breakdown.recommended_fix: Move permission enforcement to the shared tool-dispatch boundary, or add equivalent checks to every sequential, concurrent, scheduled, and delegated execution path before tool invocation.

penalty_breakdown.title: Hardcoded secret or API key detected
penalty_breakdown.recommended_fix: Move credential to environment variable or secrets manager (e.g., AWS Secrets Manager, Doppler). Add pre-commit hook to block secret commits.

penalty_breakdown.title: Internal orchestration sprawl detected
penalty_breakdown.recommended_fix: Collapse overlapping coordination layers where possible. Keep one clear main loop, minimize hidden fallback paths, and document which module owns planning, routing, and retries.

penalty_breakdown.title: Memory freshness / generation confusion detected
penalty_breakdown.recommended_fix: Define one authoritative current memory surface and make archives or summaries explicitly secondary. Rename or retire overlapping generations so humans and agents can tell what is fresh.

penalty_breakdown.title: Role-play handoff orchestration detected
penalty_breakdown.recommended_fix: Keep one agent or loop responsible for the full user intent. Use subagents for independent evidence gathering or context isolation, then merge results back to the intent owner. Convert stable, bounded steps into tools instead of giving every tool a role identity.

penalty_breakdown.title: Runtime surface sprawl detected
penalty_breakdown.recommended_fix: Reduce the number of runtime surfaces each developer must hold in their head. Document the primary runtime path and separate optional services or deployment layers more clearly.

penalty_breakdown.title: Startup surface sprawl detected
penalty_breakdown.recommended_fix: Choose one canonical startup path for development and one for background/service operation. Reduce wrapper layers and document the exact order in which launchers delegate to each other.

penalty_breakdown.title: Duplicated skill / SOP artifacts detected
penalty_breakdown.recommended_fix: Pick one canonical skill or SOP per task shape. Archive or delete duplicated variants and keep version history in Git rather than in multiple near-identical files.

findings.title: Hardcoded secret or API key detected
findings.symptom: Secret pattern found at test_redact.py:70: text = 'MY_SECRET_TOKEN="supersecretvalue123456789"'
findings.user_impact: Exposed credentials can be stolen from version control or file dumps, leading to unauthorized access and billing abuse.
findings.mechanism: Regex match for pattern: (?i)(?:api[_-]?key|apikey|secret[_-]?key|token)\s*[=:]\s*['\"]([a-zA-Z0-9+/]{20,}={0,2})['\"]
findings.root_cause: Credentials hardcoded in source instead of using environment variables or a secrets manager.
findings.recommended_fix: Move credential to environment variable or secrets manager (e.g., AWS Secrets Manager, Doppler). Add pre-commit hook to block secret commits.

findings.title: Internal orchestration sprawl detected
findings.symptom: Found 19289 orchestration markers across 5 coordination categories (delegation, planning, recovery, routing, scheduling).
findings.user_impact: Too many planning, routing, delegation, scheduling, and recovery layers can make the agent harder to debug, slower to reason about, and more likely to hide internal contradictions.
findings.mechanism: Repository-wide scan for planner/router/subagent/scheduler/fallback style orchestration markers.
findings.root_cause: The agent runtime appears to coordinate work through many overlapping orchestration layers.
findings.recommended_fix: Collapse overlapping coordination layers where possible. Keep one clear main loop, minimize hidden fallback paths, and document which module owns planning, routing, and retries.

findings.title: Memory freshness / generation confusion detected
findings.symptom: Found 129 memory-like surfaces spanning 9 categories; overlapping memory stems: init, memory, plugin, readme, session.
findings.user_impact: When checkpoints, archives, summaries, histories, and session notes overlap, agents can load stale or contradictory memory and humans lose track of which memory surface is current.
findings.mechanism: Repository scan for memory/checkpoint/archive/summary/session style file families and overlapping stems.
findings.root_cause: The project appears to maintain multiple memory generations or memory surfaces without a clear freshness contract.
findings.recommended_fix: Define one authoritative current memory surface and make archives or summaries explicitly secondary. Rename or retire overlapping generations so humans and agents can tell what is fresh.

findings.title: Role-play handoff orchestration detected
findings.symptom: Found 150 role markers across 5 role categories (builder, manager, planner, researcher, reviewer) and 2145 serial handoff markers.
findings.user_impact: Agent systems that mirror company departments often look organized while losing context at each handoff. The result is local progress with global confusion: plans, reviews, and execution drift apart.
findings.mechanism: Repository-wide scan for role-labeled agents combined with handoff/pipeline language.
findings.root_cause: The design appears to model agent collaboration as a serial org chart instead of one intent owner forking independent exploration and merging evidence.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep one agent or loop responsible for the full user intent. Use subagents for independent evidence gathering or context isolation, then merge results back to the intent owner. Convert stable, bounded steps into tools instead of giving every tool a role identity.

findings.title: Permission policy is not enforced on all dispatch paths
findings.symptom: Detected 631 tool/command dispatch sites and permission enforcement signals, but 630 dispatch sites were not near a permission check.
findings.user_impact: A runtime can look permission-hardened while sequential, concurrent, scheduled, or delegated tool paths bypass the policy and execute high-agency actions unchecked.
findings.mechanism: Line-proximity scan for tool dispatch sites versus nearby permission enforcement calls.
findings.root_cause: Permission checks appear to be attached to some dispatch paths instead of the common tool boundary.
findings.recommended_fix: Move permission enforcement to the shared tool-dispatch boundary, or add equivalent checks to every sequential, concurrent, scheduled, and delegated execution path before tool invocation.

findings.title: Startup surface sprawl detected
findings.symptom: Found 45 startup-like files and 186 launcher/wrapper sites.
findings.user_impact: When a project can be started through many overlapping wrappers, scripts, and service managers, startup becomes slower to debug, easier to break, and harder to document correctly.
findings.mechanism: Repository scan for launcher files and wrapper chains that shell out into other launchers.
findings.root_cause: The project appears to have accumulated multiple startup paths without a clear canonical boot flow.
findings.recommended_fix: Choose one canonical startup path for development and one for background/service operation. Reduce wrapper layers and document the exact order in which launchers delegate to each other.

findings.title: Runtime surface sprawl detected
findings.symptom: Found runtime markers across 6 operating surfaces (agent_stack, ops, queue_jobs, storage, ui, web_api).
findings.user_impact: Projects that mix many runtime surfaces are harder to start, debug, document, and evolve without internal friction.
findings.mechanism: Repository scan for API/UI/queue/ops/storage/agent-runtime surfaces.
findings.root_cause: The project appears to accumulate many runtime responsibilities and deployment surfaces in one place.
findings.recommended_fix: Reduce the number of runtime surfaces each developer must hold in their head. Document the primary runtime path and separate optional services or deployment layers more clearly.

findings.title: Timer delay may exceed runtime setTimeout limits
findings.symptom: Found 2 setTimeout call(s) using delay-like variables without nearby clamp/cap logic.
findings.user_impact: Very long scheduler, heartbeat, retry, or timeout values can overflow the JavaScript timer limit and fire immediately or far earlier than intended, causing stalls, busy loops, or unexpected retries.
findings.mechanism: Static scan for setTimeout(..., variableDelay) without nearby Math.min/clamp/timerDelay helpers.
findings.root_cause: Runtime delay values are passed directly to JavaScript timers without a documented maximum cap.
findings.recommended_fix: Route long delays through a shared timer helper that clamps to the Node/JS setTimeout cap, logs truncation, and has regression tests for multi-day or extremely large durations.

findings.title: Duplicated skill / SOP artifacts detected
findings.symptom: Found 270 overlapping skill-like files across 28 duplicate groups.
findings.user_impact: Duplicated SOPs and skill files create maintenance drift, make routing less predictable, and force humans or agents to guess which version is canonical.
findings.mechanism: Grouped skill/SOP/runbook-like files by normalized stem and flagged overlapping groups.
findings.root_cause: The skill system appears to contain duplicated or version-fragmented artifacts.
findings.recommended_fix: Pick one canonical skill or SOP per task shape. Archive or delete duplicated variants and keep version history in Git rather than in multiple near-identical files.

findings.title: Memory growth without apparent limit
findings.symptom: Memory/context growth pattern in useInputHistory.ts without nearby limit/trim/expire pattern.
findings.user_impact: Unbounded memory growth can cause context window overflow, increased costs, and degraded response quality.
findings.mechanism: Growth operation detected but no limit/truncation pattern found within proximity.
findings.root_cause: Memory or context is appended without size bounds, TTL, or eviction policy.
findings.recommended_fix: For personal prototypes, this is usually a polish issue rather than an immediate blocker. Once the workflow stabilizes, add explicit retention, truncation, or TTL limits.

findings.title: Output mutation / response transformation detected
findings.symptom: Response mutation in run_agent.py: L1220: # stream chunk.  Used by the gateway timeout handler to report what the; L2909: Must stay in sync with _strip_think_blocks() tag variants.; L2953: boundary-gated (only strips when the tag sits at start-of-line or (+13 more)
findings.user_impact: Post-processing of LLM output can silently alter, censor, or inject content into responses, changing what the user sees vs. what the model produced.
findings.mechanism: Regex match for output mutation/transformation patterns.
findings.root_cause: LLM responses are modified after generation but before delivery to user.

ordered_fix_plan.why_now: CRITICAL findings should be handled before lower-severity work.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Move credential to environment variable or secrets manager (e.g., AWS Secrets Manager, Doppler). Add pre-commit hook to block secret commits.

ordered_fix_plan.why_now: HIGH findings should be handled before lower-severity work.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Collapse overlapping coordination layers where possible. Keep one clear main loop, minimize hidden fallback paths, and document which module owns planning, routing, and retries.

ordered_fix_plan.why_now: HIGH findings should be handled before lower-severity work.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Define one authoritative current memory surface and make archives or summaries explicitly secondary. Rename or retire overlapping generations so humans and agents can tell what is fresh.

ordered_fix_plan.why_now: HIGH findings should be handled before lower-severity work.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Keep one agent or loop responsible for the full user intent. Use subagents for independent evidence gathering or context isolation, then merge results back to the intent owner. Convert stable, bounded steps into tools instead of giving every tool a role identity.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Permission policy is not enforced on all dispatch paths
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: HIGH findings should be handled before lower-severity work.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Move permission enforcement to the shared tool-dispatch boundary, or add equivalent checks to every sequential, concurrent, scheduled, and delegated execution path before tool invocation.

ordered_fix_plan.why_now: HIGH findings should be handled before lower-severity work.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Choose one canonical startup path for development and one for background/service operation. Reduce wrapper layers and document the exact order in which launchers delegate to each other.

ordered_fix_plan.why_now: HIGH findings should be handled before lower-severity work.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Reduce the number of runtime surfaces each developer must hold in their head. Document the primary runtime path and separate optional services or deployment layers more clearly.
